title: Clarkia mildrediae
taxonomy: Clarkia mildrediae is classified within the family Onagraceae, commonly known as the evening primrose family. This classification places it within the order Myrtales and the class Magnoliopsida. The genus Clarkia is a group of flowering plants native to western North America, and Clarkia mildrediae is a species within this group. The species is further divided into various subspecies and varieties, each with unique characteristics. Clarkia mildrediae's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Onagraceae family.

intro: Clarkia mildrediae, also known as Farewell to Spring or Godetia, is a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been found to have potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Clarkia mildrediae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
